title: Learning Japanese
wikitext:
[]: Learning Japanese             Quote Post by ice reaper » Sat Jul 16, 2005 5:39 am does anybody lnow of a good piece of media, book, DVD etc. for learning to speak and obviously understand Japanese? i've always wanted to learn it and i noticed quite a few people here know Japanese and thought i'd ask.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sat Jul 16, 2005 5:56 am There are quite a few around. I saw one the other day, but I can not remeber the name of it.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Jul 16, 2005 6:24 pm If you want to learn how to read Japanese, I higly recommend Project LRNJ: http://lrnj.com/ Don't think this is something you're going to pick up in a weekend go. We're talking about a language with almost 100 characters in its phoenetic alphabet.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Jul 17, 2005 1:50 am The phoenetic alphabet is easy. What's hard is remembering the unpredictably plentiful meanings ascribed to various recycled words, as their means of constructing words is so much more limited than in English. Since a lot of advertising and such is in katakana, just learning the alphabet will get you where you need to go for a good while, so even just dabbling is well worth it. You'll be navigating Japanese sites with ease in no time!
